recalls.Description: DR Power has issued a recall for its 62-volt 5.0 Ah LiPRO rechargeable lithium-ion battery packs, which are used with various DR Power battery-operated tools, including lawnmowers, trimmers, and snow throwers. The recall affects standalone battery packs with model number 414240, as well as removable battery packs included with outdoor equipment models 524340, SB11022XEN, T4X3026XEN, T4X3062XENR, CE75021XEN0, and CE77021XEN0. The model number can be found on the battery pack's label, and the DR Power logo is displayed prominently on the product. It is crucial to note that these recalled batteries should not be disposed of in regular trash or recycling bins due to their fire hazard risk. Instead, consumers should check with their local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: DR Power Recalls LiPRO Lithium-Ion Battery Packs Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach DR Power at 800-454-8643 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@drpower.com, or visit https://drpower.com/recalls and https://www.drpower.com/ and select 'Product Recalls.'

Products.Name: DR Power LiPRO 5.0 Ah Lithium-Ion Battery Packs

Images.Caption: Recalled DR Power Model 414240 Battery Pack (5 Ah)
DR Power Model 524340, DR Trimmer Mower
DR Power Model SB11022XEN, DR Pulse 62V Snow Thrower
DR Power Model T4X3062XEN, DR Trimmer Pulse
DR Power Model CE75021XEN0, DR Pulse 62V Mower 21"
DR Power Model CE77021XEN0, DR Pulse 62V Mower 21" SP

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of incidents of fire and/or overheating. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Ningbo New Team Import & Export Co. Ltd. ("Lera"), of China

Retailers.Name: Available at home improvement and hardware stores across the country, as well as online at DRPower.com and CountryHomeProducts.com from April 2018 through July 2024, priced between $300 and $700 for outdoor equipment including battery packs, and $250 for individual battery packs.

Importers.Name: Generac Power Systems Inc., of Waukesha, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards from short-circuiting battery packs

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled battery packs and contact DR Power for a pro-rated refund. Proof of destruction is required via two photographs uploaded on the recall website, along with the completion of the online recall form at https://www.drpower.com/5AHB-recall. Dispose of the battery in accordance with local regulations if necessary.

recalls.metaDescription: DR Power recalls LiPRO lithium-ion battery packs due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a refund and dispose of the product safely.

recalls.Description: Ariens has issued a recall for specific models of their DELUXE, PLATINUM, and PROFESSIONAL-series Sno-Thro® snow throwers. These units, available in bright orange, may feature either rear wheels or tracks. The model and serial numbers can be found on the white square ID tag attached to the frame. Some of these snow throwers have already undergone inspection and repair. Consumers are encouraged to visit https://www.ariensco.com/safetyrecall to check if their unit is affected.
recalls.Title: Ariens Snow Throwers Recalled for Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Ariens Company at 877-740-7060, available from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.ariensco.com/safetyrecall or www.ariens.com and select 'Safety Recalls' for further details.

Products.Name: Ariens DELUXE, PLATINUM, and PROFESSIONAL-series Snow Throwers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 6,260 (In addition, about 1,100 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Ariens Snow Throwers with Tracks
Recalled Ariens Snow Thrower with Wheels
Location of Model and Serial Number on the frame of the recalled Snow Throwers

Retailers.Name: Available through authorized Ariens dealers across the country, as well as Ace Hardware, Blain's Farm & Fleet, Do It Best, Family Farm & Home, Mills Fleet Farm, Northern Tool and Equipment, Lowe's, and True Value. Also sold online at AceHardware.com, Farmandfleet.com, Doitbest.com, familyfarmandhome.com, Lowes.com, fleetfarm.com, northerntool.com, and TrueValue.com from August 2024 to October 2024, priced between $1,770 and $5,440.

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration due to the impeller and auger assembly continuing to rotate after the control lever is released.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled snow throwers immediately and reach out to Ariens Company or an authorized dealer for a complimentary repair at their local dealership. Ariens and its dealers will verify if a unit has been inspected or repaired and will carry out necessary repairs on affected models.

recalls.metaDescription: Ariens recalls select snow throwers due to a laceration hazard. Check if your unit is affected and learn how to get a free repair.
